title: Modifying Data Frames
description: Add, remove, and rename columns; modify values; handle missing data.

slides.title: Adding Columns ➕
slides.content:
You can add a new column using `$` or `[`:

```r
df$new_col <- values
df[["new_col"]] <- values
df["new_col"] <- list(values)  # or a vector of same length
```

The new column must have the same number of rows (or be recycled).

slides.title: Add a Column
slides.codeSnippet:
df <- data.frame(name=c("A","B","C"), score=c(85,90,95))
df$grade <- c("B", "A", "A")
df[["passed"]] <- c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE)
print(df)

quizData.question: What happens if you try to add a column with a length different from the number of rows?

options.text: Recycling occurs (with a warning if not multiple)

quizData.explanation: R will recycle the shorter vector, issuing a warning if the longer length is not a multiple.

slides.title: Removing Columns ✂️
slides.content:
Set a column to `NULL` to remove it.

```r
df$grade <- NULL
df[["passed"]] <- NULL
```

You can also assign `NULL` to a column using `[` with a list: `df["grade"] <- list(NULL)`.

slides.title: Remove a Column
slides.codeSnippet:
df <- data.frame(x=1:3, y=4:6, z=7:9)
df$y <- NULL
print(df)
df["z"] <- list(NULL)
print(df)

slides.title: Modifying Values
slides.content:
You can change individual cells or whole columns by assignment:

```r
df[2, "score"] <- 100   # change a single value
df$score <- df$score * 1.1   # modify entire column
```

slides.title: Modify Values
slides.codeSnippet:
df <- data.frame(name=c("A","B","C"), score=c(85,90,95))
df[1, "score"] <- 95
df$score <- df$score + 5
print(df)

slides.title: Handling Missing Data
slides.content:
Use `is.na()` to find missing values. You can replace NA values with something else:

```r
df$score[is.na(df$score)] <- 0
```
